packs.name: HAProxy
packs.description: Protects against destructive HAProxy load balancer operations like stopping the service or disabling backends via runtime API.

structural_patterns.name: haproxy-soft-stop
structural_patterns.reason: haproxy -sf sends a soft stop signal, terminating the load balancer gracefully.

structural_patterns.name: haproxy-hard-stop
structural_patterns.reason: haproxy -st sends a hard stop signal, immediately terminating the load balancer.

structural_patterns.name: haproxy-systemctl-stop
structural_patterns.reason: systemctl stop haproxy stops the HAProxy service.

structural_patterns.name: haproxy-service-stop
structural_patterns.reason: service haproxy stop stops the HAProxy service.

structural_patterns.name: haproxy-socat-disable-server
structural_patterns.reason: Disabling a server via HAProxy runtime API removes it from the load balancer pool.

structural_patterns.name: haproxy-socat-shutdown-sessions
structural_patterns.reason: Shutting down sessions via HAProxy runtime API terminates active connections.

structural_patterns.name: haproxy-socat-disable-frontend
structural_patterns.reason: Disabling a frontend via HAProxy runtime API stops accepting new connections.

structural_patterns.name: haproxy-socat-shutdown-frontend
structural_patterns.reason: Shutting down a frontend via HAProxy runtime API terminates it immediately.

structural_patterns.name: haproxy-config-delete
structural_patterns.reason: Removing files from /etc/haproxy deletes HAProxy configuration.

packs.name: nginx
packs.description: Protects against destructive nginx load balancer operations like stopping the service or deleting config files.

structural_patterns.name: nginx-stop
structural_patterns.reason: nginx -s stop shuts down nginx and stops the load balancer.
structural_patterns.explanation:
Sending the stop signal terminates nginx immediately without waiting for active connections to finish. All in-flight requests are dropped and upstream traffic stops being routed.

To reload config without downtime:
  nginx -s reload

To gracefully finish current requests:
  nginx -s quit

structural_patterns.name: nginx-quit
structural_patterns.reason: nginx -s quit gracefully stops nginx and halts traffic handling.
structural_patterns.explanation:
The quit signal waits for active connections to complete before shutting down nginx. While graceful, it still permanently stops the load balancer and no new connections are accepted once issued.

To reload config without stopping:
  nginx -s reload

Test config before changes:
  nginx -t

structural_patterns.name: systemctl-stop-nginx
structural_patterns.reason: systemctl stop nginx stops the nginx service and disrupts traffic.
structural_patterns.explanation:
Stopping the nginx systemd service shuts down all nginx worker processes. Any sites, APIs, or reverse proxies served by this nginx instance become unreachable until the service is restarted.

Check status first:
  systemctl status nginx

To restart instead of stop:
  systemctl restart nginx

structural_patterns.name: service-stop-nginx
structural_patterns.reason: service nginx stop stops the nginx service and disrupts traffic.
structural_patterns.explanation:
Stopping nginx via the legacy service command terminates all worker processes. All sites and proxies served by nginx become unavailable.

Check status first:
  service nginx status

Prefer systemctl on systemd systems:
  systemctl status nginx

structural_patterns.name: nginx-config-delete
structural_patterns.reason: Removing files from /etc/nginx deletes nginx configuration.
structural_patterns.explanation:
Deleting nginx configuration files removes site definitions, upstream blocks, SSL certificate references, and load balancing rules. Nginx will fail to start or reload without valid configuration.

Back up config first:
  cp -r /etc/nginx /etc/nginx.backup

Test config validity:
  nginx -t

packs.name: Traefik
packs.description: Protects against destructive Traefik load balancer operations like stopping containers, deleting config, or API deletions.

structural_patterns.name: traefik-docker-stop
structural_patterns.reason: Stopping the Traefik container halts all traffic routing.
structural_patterns.explanation:
Stopping or killing the Traefik container immediately halts all HTTP/HTTPS traffic routing. Every service behind this load balancer becomes unreachable until Traefik is restarted.

Check container status first:
  docker inspect traefik

View logs for issues:
  docker logs traefik

structural_patterns.name: traefik-docker-rm
structural_patterns.reason: Removing the Traefik container destroys the load balancer.
structural_patterns.explanation:
Removing the Traefik container deletes it entirely, including any runtime state, Let's Encrypt certificates stored in the container, and dynamic configuration. The container must be recreated from scratch.

Stop without removing:
  docker stop traefik

Back up certificates first if stored in container volume.

structural_patterns.name: traefik-compose-down
structural_patterns.reason: docker-compose down on Traefik stops and removes the load balancer.
structural_patterns.explanation:
Running docker-compose down stops and removes Traefik containers, networks, and optionally volumes. All traffic routing stops and any non-persisted state is lost.

To stop without removing:
  docker-compose stop traefik

To restart cleanly:
  docker-compose restart traefik

structural_patterns.name: traefik-kubectl-delete-pod
structural_patterns.reason: Deleting Traefik pods/deployments disrupts traffic routing.
structural_patterns.explanation:
Deleting Traefik pods or deployments removes the load balancer from the cluster. If managed by a Deployment, pods will be recreated but with downtime. Deleting the Deployment itself stops all traffic routing until it is redeployed.

Check pod status:
  kubectl get pods -l app=traefik

Restart pods without deleting:
  kubectl rollout restart deployment traefik

structural_patterns.name: traefik-kubectl-delete-ingressroute
structural_patterns.reason: Deleting IngressRoute CRDs removes Traefik routing rules.
structural_patterns.explanation:
IngressRoute custom resources define how Traefik routes traffic to backend services. Deleting them removes those routing rules, making the associated services unreachable through the load balancer.

List current routes first:
  kubectl get ingressroute

Describe a route before deleting:
  kubectl describe ingressroute <name>

structural_patterns.name: traefik-config-delete
structural_patterns.reason: Removing Traefik config files disrupts load balancer configuration.
structural_patterns.explanation:
Deleting Traefik configuration files removes entrypoint definitions, middleware chains, TLS settings, and provider configurations. Traefik will fail to start or lose routing rules on the next reload.

Back up config first:
  cp traefik.yml traefik.yml.backup

Validate config before changes:
  traefik healthcheck

structural_patterns.name: traefik-api-delete
structural_patterns.reason: DELETE operations against Traefik API can remove routing configuration.
structural_patterns.explanation:
Sending DELETE requests to the Traefik API removes routers, services, or middleware from the running configuration. Depending on the provider, these changes may be permanent or reverted on restart.

Use GET to inspect before deleting:
  curl -X GET http://traefik:8080/api/overview

structural_patterns.name: traefik-systemctl-stop
structural_patterns.reason: systemctl stop traefik stops the Traefik service.
structural_patterns.explanation:
Stopping the Traefik systemd service shuts down the load balancer process. All HTTP/HTTPS traffic routing ceases until the service is restarted.

Check status first:
  systemctl status traefik

To restart instead:
  systemctl restart traefik

structural_patterns.name: traefik-service-stop
structural_patterns.reason: service traefik stop stops the Traefik service.
structural_patterns.explanation:
Stopping Traefik via the legacy service command terminates the load balancer. All traffic routing stops until the service is manually restarted.

Check status first:
  service traefik status

Prefer systemctl on systemd systems:
  systemctl status traefik

packs.name: AWS ELB
packs.description: Protects against destructive AWS Elastic Load Balancing (ELB/ALB/NLB) operations like deleting load balancers, target groups, or deregistering targets from live traffic.

structural_patterns.name: elbv2-delete-load-balancer
structural_patterns.reason: aws elbv2 delete-load-balancer permanently deletes the load balancer.

structural_patterns.name: elbv2-delete-target-group
structural_patterns.reason: aws elbv2 delete-target-group permanently deletes the target group.

structural_patterns.name: elbv2-deregister-targets
structural_patterns.reason: aws elbv2 deregister-targets removes targets from the load balancer, impacting live traffic.

structural_patterns.name: elbv2-delete-listener
structural_patterns.reason: aws elbv2 delete-listener deletes a listener, potentially breaking traffic routing.

structural_patterns.name: elbv2-delete-rule
structural_patterns.reason: aws elbv2 delete-rule deletes a listener rule, potentially breaking routing.

structural_patterns.name: elb-delete-load-balancer
structural_patterns.reason: aws elb delete-load-balancer permanently deletes the classic load balancer.

structural_patterns.name: elb-deregister-instances
structural_patterns.reason: aws elb deregister-instances-from-load-balancer removes instances from the load balancer, impacting live traffic.
